title: Joey Lussick

[[Freddy Lussick]] (brother) }} }} '''Joey Lussick''' (born 28 December 1995) is a former Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He previously played for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly Warringah]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[St. Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Lussick was born in [[Freshwater, New South Wales]], [[Australia]]. He is the younger brother of former Sea Eagles teammate [[Darcy Lussick]]{{cite web|author=1 hr ago |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-turning-tables-on-manlys-poaching-of-player-talent-with-interest-20120330-1w3it.html |title=Parramatta turning tables on Manly's poaching of player talent, with interest |publisher=Smh.com.au |date=2012-03-31 |accessdate=2017-07-23}} the older brother of [[Freddy Lussick]] and son of former Sea Eagles player Jason Lussick.{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/daddy-of-all-tough-guys-meet-the-man-who-moulded-darcy-lussick-20111001-1l2nq.html |title=Daddy of all tough guys: Meet the man who moulded Darcy Lussick |publisher=Smh.com.au |date=2011-10-02 |accessdate=2017-07-23}} Lussick played his junior rugby league for the [[Beacon Hill Bears]], and rugby union for [[Newington College]] and the Australian Schoolboys rugby league and union team before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{cn|date=June 2025}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== In late 2013, Lussick played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=25953696|title=Australians Win Second Test - Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League - FOX SPORTS PULSE|publisher=foxsportspulse.com|accessdate=2014-07-25}} In 2014 and 2015, he played for the Parramatta Eels' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/l/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150616162758/http://nycdatabase.org/players/l/ |url-status=usurped |archive-date=16 June 2015 |title=L |publisher=Nyc Database |date= |accessdate=2017-07-23}} During 2015, he made a mid-season switch to the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-nrl-and-nyc-team-lists-finals-week-1/tabid/10874/newsid/89622/default.aspx |title=Updated NRL and NYC team lists: Finals Week 1 |publisher=NRL.com |date=2015-09-13 |accessdate=2017-07-23}} In 2016, he graduated to the Roosters' [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] team, [[Wyong Roos]].{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/03/01/teams_intrust_super_.html |title=TEAMS | Intrust Super Premiership Rd 1 |publisher=NSWRL |date= March 2016|accessdate=2017-07-23}} ===2017=== In 2017, Lussick joined [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]]. In round 20 of the [[2017 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Manly against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]],{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-dragons-v-sea-eagles/tabid/10874/newsid/110203/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Dragons v Sea Eagles |publisher=NRL.com |date= 23 July 2017|accessdate=2017-07-23}}{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2017/07/23/nrl_late_mail_round_.html |title=NRL Late Mail | Round 20 v Dragons |publisher=Sea Eagles |date= 23 July 2017|accessdate=2017-07-23}} playing alongside his brother Darcy and scoring a try.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/points-galore-as-dragons-thump-manly/tabid/10874/newsid/110283/default.aspx |title=Points galore as Dragons thump Manly |publisher=NRL.com |date= 23 July 2017|accessdate=2017-07-23}} ===2018=== In 2018, Lussick joined English side, the [[Salford Red Devils]] for the rest of the season in their aim to remain in the Super League during the Middle 8s.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/44984642|title=Joey Lussick: Salford Red Devils sign hooker from Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|work=BBC Sport |date=27 July 2018|publisher=|accessdate=28 July 2018}} His existing footballing relationship with fellow new Red Devil [[Jackson Hastings]], having played Australian schoolboys as well as at the Roosters and Manly with Hastings, was said to be an added bonus according to Head Coach Ian Watson, as was his adaptability to play anywhere in the spine.{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/salford-get-joey-lussick-over-the-line/|title=Salford get Joey Lussick over the line - Salford Red Devils|date=27 July 2018|publisher=|accessdate=28 July 2018}} ===2019=== Lussick was part of the Salford side which surprised many by reaching the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] against St Helens. Salford went into the game looking to win their first championship since 1976 but faced a St Helens team which had only lost 3 games all season. Lussick played in the final which was won by St Helens by a score of 23–6 at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|last1=Unwin |first1=Will |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian |date=12 October 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}} ===2020=== On 22 October, Lussick signed a one-year deal to join [[NRL]] side [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] for the [[2021 NRL season]].{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-parramatta-eels-rugby-league-brad-arthur-signings-contracts-tom-opacic-isaiah-papalii-joey-lussick/oefimmxorje714od1x995mipr/|title=Parramatta Eels sign Tom Opacic, Isaiah Papali'i and Joey Lussick for 2021 season|website=www.sportingnews.com |date=21 October 2020 }} ===2021=== In round 11 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Lussick made his club debut for Parramatta in their 28-6 loss against Manly-Warringah.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/23/right-edge-review-after-blake-badly-exposed-by-manly/|title=Right-edge review after Blake badly exposed by Manly|website=www.nrl.com |date=23 May 2021 }} On 16 September, he signed a three-year deal to join English side [[St Helens RFC]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-st-georgeillawarra-dragons-gold-coast-titans-departing-players-corey-norman-ashley-taylor-tyrone-peachey-transfer-centre-signings/news-story/a6c1cdb196e86219a8075fa120d8ae69/|title=Bulldogs' backline boost as rising star re-signs; Eels hooker seals UK exit: Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au |date=16 September 2021 }} Lussick made a total of nine appearances for Parramatta in the [[2021 NRL season]]. He did not feature in the club's finals campaign. On 22 September, he was officially released by the Parramatta club.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2021-parramatta-eels-departing-players-blake-ferguson-rugby-league-joey-lussick/1ckdao762ec5j1s1xfshi22jb0/|title=Blake Ferguson's NRL career likely over after Parramatta Eels announce departures of four players|website=www.sportingnews.com |date=22 September 2021 }} ===2022=== In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Lussick made his club debut for [[St Helens R.F.C.]] where they defeated [[Catalans Dragons]] 28-8.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-beat-catalans-in-fiery-season-opener-talking-points/|title=St Helens beat Catalans in fiery season opener – talking points|website=www.loverugbyleague.com |date=10 February 2022 }} On 24 September, Lussick played from the interchange bench in St Helens 24-12 Grand Final victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].{{cite web|last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com |date=24 September 2022 }} ===2023=== On 18 February 2023, Lussick played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}} Following St Helens Challenge Cup semi-final loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]], Lussick announced that he would be departing the club with immediate effect after signing with an undisclosed NRL club.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66277843|title=Joey Lussick: St Helens hooker to return to Australia with immediate effect after signing for NRL club|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=22 July 2023 }} On 25 July, it was announced that Lussick had re-joined Parramatta on a three-year deal which would keep him at the club until the end of the 2025 season.{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/signing-news-2023-joey-lussick-joins-parramatta-eels-josh-hodgson-injury/f900a54b-f6af-4b8c-9c2b-bd15528047ce/|title=Eels sign Super League hooker as Josh Hodgson heads towards medical retirement|website=wwos.nine.com.au |date=25 July 2023 }} ===2024=== Lussick played 20 matches for Parramatta in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 15th on the table. Lussick had started out as the clubs first choice hooker at the start of the season but due to indifferent performances was demoted to the interchange bench or the NSW Cup in place of [[Brendan Hands]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-parramatta-eels-season-review-reagan-campbell-gillard-future-20240907-p5k8qj.html/|title=The Mole's end of season review: What went wrong at the Parramatta Eels?|website=www.nine.com.au |date=7 September 2024 }} ===2025=== Lussick was limited to only four games with the Parramatta side in the [[2025 NRL season]] after falling down the pecking order because of an elbow injury. In early September, it was announced that Lussick would be departing Parramatta after not being offered a new contract.{{cite web|url=https://www.msn.com/en-au/sport/football/parramatta-eels-confirm-four-new-names-on-departing-players-list/ar-AA1M20sh/|title=Parramatta Eels confirm four new names on departing players list|website=www.msn.com}} ===2026=== On 18 January, Lussick announced his retirement from rugby league.{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2026-01-18 |title=Former St Helens title winner announces retirement aged just 30 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joey-lussick-st-helens-retirement |access-date=2026-01-18 |website=Love Rugby League |language=en}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Goals !Pts |- |2017 |{{Leagueicon|Manly|16}} [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] |1 |1 | | |- |2018 | rowspan="3" |{{leagueicon|Salford city|12}} [[Salford Red Devils]] |7 |2 | |8 |- |2019 |34 |13 |4 |64 |- |2020 |18 |3 | |12 |- |2021 |{{Leagueicon|Parramatta|16}} [[Parramatta Eels]] |9 |2 | |8 |- |2022 |{{leagueicon|St Helens|12}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |32 |5 |1 |22 |- | rowspan="2" |2023 |{{leagueicon|St Helens|12}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |20 |6 |7 |38 |- |{{Leagueicon|Parramatta|16}} [[Parramatta Eels]] |5 | | | |- |2024 | rowspan="2" |{{Leagueicon|Parramatta|16}} [[Parramatta Eels]] |20 |5 | |20 |- |2025 |2 | | | |- | |Totals |147 |37 |12 |172 |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category}} *[https://www.salfordreddevils.net/team/joey-lussick/ Salford Red Devils profile] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/470/joey-lussick SL profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20170929045452/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/team/profiles/joey_lussick.html Manly Warringah Sea Eagles profile] {{St Helens -
